name: Collab Ventures
text_content:
The call will focus on Kinship Ventures’ capital raise and suitability for Collab Ventures’ GCC and Middle East network
Collab Ventures screens fund managers for family offices aiming to diversify portfolios in the next 6–10 months
They are actively engaging managers seeking $100–350M raises, with deployments starting October 2025
Expect questions about fund size, hard cap, current assets under management, whether you’re targeting the Middle East, structure for regional investors, upcoming trips to the region, and if you already have LPs in the GCC
Call purpose is to see alignment and potential introduction to their LPs, who pay Collab to screen managers, while managers pay a one-time fee for capital introductions

Other contacts
Jatin Seervi jatins@collabventures.ae
Ripunjita Sharma ripunjitas@collabventures.ae

Dear Moj,

Pleasure e-connecting with you!

A short intro about us:
We are an Abu Dhabi based Family Office owned business. Our primary role is to screen fund managers across various asset classes and sectors globally. We act as frontline advisors to these investors from the GCC region who are actively looking at diversifying their portfolio in the next 6-10 months’ time frame. The aim is to pre select and qualify the right set of managers for them and help connect in order to raise capital.
 
We are currently engaging in conversation with active managers looking to raise a minimum of 100-350 million USD in the next 4 to 8 months’ timeframe. The capital deployment starts from Oct 2025. Minimum ticket size ranges from 2 to 5 million USD.

It is a dual partnership. We charge a nominal fee to the LPs who work with us to screen potential managers. The managers who work with us to source new capital pay us a one time fee.
 
I have a few questions before we schedule the call so that our time is well spent.
 
1.⁠ ⁠Are you currently raising capital? 
2.⁠ ⁠Is the Middle East a key target region for your fundraising efforts? 
3.⁠ ⁠What is the total fund size, including the hard cap? 
4.⁠ ⁠What is the firm's current assets under management (AUM)? 
5.⁠ ⁠Have you established an offshore feeder fund to accommodate Middle Eastern investors while ensuring no tax implications for them? 
6.⁠ ⁠Do you have any planned business trips to the Middle East this year?
7. Do you currently have any LPs from the region? Yes/No (no names required)
 
Kindly let me know and we can schedule a call based on the details.
 
Look forward to hearing back from you.

Best,
Jatin Seervi

name: Max Khalkhali
text_content:
Company: GEX

Updates:
October 2 meeting - committed 1M

Max @ GEX <> Moj @ Kinship - October 03
VIEW RECORDING: https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz
Meeting Purpose

Introductory call between Max Khalkhali (potential LP) and Moj Mahdara (GP at Kinship Ventures) to discuss Kinship's fund strategy and explore potential investment.

Key Takeaways

  - Kinship Ventures: Consumer-focused fund targeting $75-100M, led by 3 GPs (Moj Mahdara, Gwyneth Paltrow, Abe Burns)
  - Strategy: 25-30 companies, $500K-$2.5M checks, 5% ownership target, focus on sub-$20M valuations
  - Max's interest: Considering $1M investment in Q1 2024, sees Kinship as potential consumer play in his portfolio
  - Both parties expressed mutual interest in further discussions and due diligence

Topics

Kinship Ventures Overview

  - Founded by Moj Mahdara, Gwyneth Paltrow, and Abe Burns
  - Consumer-focused fund targeting $75-100M
  - 3 GPs: Moj (full-time), Gwyneth and Abe (part-time)
  - Portfolio construction: 25-30 companies, $500K-$2.5M checks, 5% ownership target
  - Focus on sub-$20M valuations, primarily seed to Series A
  - Areas of interest: consumer tech, future of work, family tech, supply chain innovation

Max Khalkhali's Investment Strategy

  - Allocating $30M across different VC categories:
      - 30% to emerging managers
      - 40% to established but not top-tier VCs
      - 30% to larger funds through private wealth channels
  - Targeting $1M checks for emerging managers, aiming for 5-6 total
  - Currently building positions in deep tech, healthcare/biotech, and software
  - Seeking a consumer play, which Kinship could potentially fill

Mutual Due Diligence

  - Moj offered to connect Max with other Kinship GPs
  - Max requested to be added to Kinship's LP communications
  - Both agreed to continue discussions and information sharing over the next few months

Industry Insights

  - Discussion on current market conditions and valuation practices
  - Moj's perspective on LA's emerging tech scene and potential to surpass NYC
  - Shared views on the need for market adjustments and realistic valuations

Next Steps

  - Moj to set up a call between Max and Kinship GPs Gwyneth and Abe
  - Moj to add Max to Kinship's LP communications and updates
  - Max to review Kinship materials and continue due diligence
  - Both parties to reconnect in February/March 2024 for potential investment decision
Action Items
  - Schedule call: Max + Gwyneth/Abe - WATCH (30 secs): https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz?timestamp=1826.9999
  - Send inflection AI investment memo to Max - WATCH (30 secs): https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz?timestamp=1925.9999
  - Review Kinship Ventures' inflection AI memo - WATCH (30 secs): https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz?timestamp=1925.9999
  - Add Max to LP comms/newsletter lists - WATCH (30 secs): https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz?timestamp=1982.9999
  - Evaluate Kinship Ventures for Feb/Mar investment - WATCH (30 secs): https://fathom.video/share/THvRAeNmYeS1DPDJB3uzcGrsyduyHvJz?timestamp=2003.9999
